title: Level 647
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 647 : "The Grave Of The Ferries"}} {{Background|file=Shopferry.jpg}} {{Class Undetermined}} '''Level 647''' is the 648th level of the Backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=Shopferry.jpg|caption=An image of one of the shops found on Level 647. Photographer dead.}} ==Description== '''Level 647''', otherwise known as '''The Grave Of The Ferries''', is an expansive non-Euclidean space resembling Finnish car ferries from the 1980s. The level is in an almost constant pitch darkness with the windows of the level showing an expansive body of water shrouded in darkness outside. The temperature of the level most of the time is at a stable and cool 12°C. The only way to light up the level is using flashlights which can be found on the found lying on the floor or on the tables of the restaurants and bars found on the level. Also, rarely there are small areas found in the level which contain working lights. The parts with functioning lights found in them are much less decayed compared to parts of the level without functional lights. The level spans practically infinitely, with a lot of areas in the level leading to dead ends and the level constantly changing in its structure, making it impossible to map and so that finding other wanderers is extremely rare to impossible. The main way of going through the level is by walking through long spanning hallways which feature numerous areas found in car ferries around the Baltic Sea in the 80s, like bars, restaurants and shops on the side of the hallways. These areas are completely devoid of most furniture and supplies, with the shops having completely empty shelves. The level looks to be in a state of decay, with the wallpapers peeling off the walls in some parts of the level and mold growing on some surfaces found on the level. There is also a distinct smell of metal and paint present in some parts of the level. It is advised to leave an area where there is a smell of paint and metal due to it being able to cause hallucinatory effects and intense headaches. When entering the level a wanderer will immediately start to experience the symptoms of severe paranoia which may include hallucinations, delusions, hypervigilance, and constant distrust. It is recommended to bring almond water to this level as sanity will suffer due to the paranoic effects caused by the level. The reason for the severe paranoia is not known yet but it has been theorised that there is a entity causing this. {{Blurimage|file=Level647_photoone.jpg|caption=An image of a information desk found on the level. Photographer missing.}} There have also been reports of hearing various ship announcements in languages such as Finnish, Swedish, Estonian and sometimes in English coming from far away on the level. Wanderers who have escaped the level have also reported hearing screaming along the hallways and water mysteriously flowing down the walls in certain parts of the level. Occasionally when wandering through the level, one might find staircases that can bring a person to a car deck or some upper parts of the level. In these staircase areas there are elevators next to the staircases that do not seem to work, and there are deck plans seen on the wall next to the elevator that display various deck plans for ferries of the 80s which display the ship shown on the deck plan having infinite decks. The car deck of the level features a standard car deck found on the Baltic Sea car ferries of the 80s with seemingly abandoned cars being found there. The car deck is one of the few areas with a finite size in the level with the area being only about 205 meters in length. The both ends of the car deck feature massive closed doors which can be used to get cars off and on the car deck. These doors are strangely wielded shut and cannot be opened. Entering the car deck on Level 647 is not recommended, as most who have entered the area have never returned. The few who have returned have reported hearing tapping noises on the walls of the car deck and sounds of crying and screaming being heard all over the car deck. Rarely going up many of the staircases may lead a person to a rumored outside area, which has been described as being an open deck area with a presence of a dark fog being around the outside are at all times. {{Blurimage|file=Cardeck 647.jpg|caption=An image of the car deck on level 647. Photographer missing.}} Wandering through the level there may appear text on the walls of the level that might spell out the wanderer's name or some personal information that only the wanderer knows of. Sometimes wandering through the hallways of the level there can be found doors that have a crew only sign on top of them. Going through these doors will lead to an expansive crew only area which features storage rooms, crew lounges, crew cabins and more areas found on the crew areas of ships found in [[The Frontrooms]]. Most storage rooms are unlocked, but some are locked and are impossible to open by any means. These storage rooms often feature broken machinery and rotten food. In the crew only areas classical music similar to the ones heard in the cabin hallways can be heard. This music is much louder compared to the cabin hallways and can be heard playing from locked storage rooms. Usually going to the upper decks of the level leads to hallways of cabins spanning for kilometers. In the cabin hallways there is a constant silent sound of slowed down classical music playing somewhere in the hallways. The music will always be playing from every direction, and it never gets quieter or louder. It is unknown where this music is coming from, but it is rumored to come from the crew only areas of the level. The doors of these cabins are usually locked, but those that are unlocked usually have an interior of a standard 2-4 person cabin, where it is recommended to take a quick rest before getting out of the area quickly, as the cabin hallways of '''Level 647''' have a memory-altering effect akin to dementia that may cause a person to start to get the effects of memory loss after 14 hours of being inside the cabin hallways. The reason for the memory loss is theorized to be due to the classical music heard in the hallways. {{Blurimage|file=Cabin_Hallwaylevel_647.jpg|caption=An image of a cabin hallway found in the level. Photographer alive.}} ==Entities== As the level is still largely unknown and unexplored, there are not confirmed entities at the moment, although there are numerous unofficial reports of tall, smiling humanoid creatures being seen wandering the hallways of the level or sleeping in the cabins of the level. Accounts for these unconfirmed entities have noted that they have been hostile towards humans and that they like to spectate, tease, and hunt humans, as there are no viable food sources in the level. It is recommended to report any sightings of entities to any [[M.E.G.]] operatives you might stumble upon after exiting the level. Most entity sightings in this level have been written off as hallucinations due to the severe paranoia present on a person at all times in the level. ==Colonies and Outposts== There are no colonies and outposts due to the non-Euclidean and isolating structure of the level. ==Gallery== File:Robert cafe 647.jpg|A cafe found on the level next to a hallway File:Fantaasia 1979.jpg|A dimly lit area of the level File:Storageroomlevel 647.jpg|A storage room found in the crew only areas File:Abandonedrestaurant 647.png|A more decayed part of the level
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== * Seeing a ferry on [[Level 7]] and climbing through a set of ladders on the side of the ship onto the open deck will cause one to noclip into this level. *Following signs pointing to a ferry terminal on [[Level 43]] will lead to this level. *Noclipping into a advertisement for a "Backrooms Ferry Line cruise" on [[Level 17]] will lead to this level ===Exits=== * Extremely rarely seeing and following signs that point to a cargo hold will lead one to [[Level 17]] * Jumping into the darkness seen outside the level by breaking the windows will cause one to lose consciousness and wake up in [[Level 135]] *There is a minimal chance to suddenly get knocked out and sent to [[Level 7]]
[[Level 647/offset|'''The Incident.''']]
